theory.intro: Triangles can be named by comparing the lengths of their sides. Equal sides are shown with matching tick marks.
facts: **Equilateral**: all three sides equal (all three angles equal too).
**Scalene**: all three sides different — no equal sides.
steps: Look at the tick marks (or given lengths) on each side.
Count how many sides are equal.
Three equal $\Rightarrow$ equilateral; two equal $\Rightarrow$ isosceles; none equal $\Rightarrow$ scalene.

development.q: A triangle has sides $8$ cm, $8$ cm and $8$ cm. Name it and state how many sides are equal.
Classify this triangle by its sides.
Classify this triangle by its sides.
Two sides of an isosceles triangle are $7$ cm. The perimeter is $20$ cm. Find the third side.
Can a triangle be both equilateral and isosceles? Explain briefly.
development.a: Yes — an equilateral triangle has (at least) two equal sides

development.q: Classify this triangle by its sides.
A triangle has sides $9$ cm, $12$ cm and $9$ cm. Name it.
Classify this triangle by its sides.
An equilateral triangle has perimeter $21$ cm. How long is each side?
A triangle has sides $5$ cm, $5$ cm and $9$ cm. True or false: it is scalene.

mastery.q: A triangle has sides $x$, $x$ and $10$ cm with perimeter $26$ cm. Find $x$ and name the triangle.
An isosceles triangle has a perimeter of $34$ cm. Two equal sides are each $13$ cm. Find the base.
A triangle has angles $40^{\circ}$, $70^{\circ}$ and $70^{\circ}$. Name it by its sides.
A scalene triangle has sides $6$ cm, $11$ cm and one more side. Could the third side be $11$ cm? Explain.
mastery.a: No — that would make two sides equal (isosceles)

mastery.q: A triangle's perimeter is $36$ cm and all sides are equal. Find each side and name it.
An isosceles triangle has equal sides twice the base. If the base is $5$ cm, find the perimeter.
A triangle has sides $8$ cm and $8$ cm. The third side is a whole number. List the names it could be.
mastery.a: Isosceles (any third side), or equilateral if the third side is $8$ cm

mastery.q: The perimeter of an equilateral triangle equals the perimeter of a $6$ cm by $9$ cm rectangle. Find one side of the triangle.
Classify and find the unknown side.
